+import type { LayoutData, MermaidConfig } from 'mermaid';
+import type { Bounds, Point } from 'mermaid/src/types.js';
+import { BoundingBox, Layout } from 'non-layered-tidy-tree-layout';
+import type {
+  Edge,
+  LayoutResult,
+  Node,
+  PositionedEdge,
+  PositionedNode,
+  TidyTreeNode,
+} from './types.js';
+
+/**
+ * Execute the tidy-tree layout algorithm on generic layout data
+ *
+ * This function takes layout data and uses the non-layered-tidy-tree-layout
+ * algorithm to calculate optimal node positions for tree structures.
+ *
+ * @param data - The layout data containing nodes, edges, and configuration
+ * @param config - Mermaid configuration object
+ * @returns Promise resolving to layout result with positioned nodes and edges
+ */
+export function executeTidyTreeLayout(
+  data: LayoutData,
+  _config: MermaidConfig

discussion_comments.comment_body: This will require update in all shapes. None of them are updated.
Yes, we should standardize the function signature for the shapes, so that it's consistent across shapes. Shape specific settings can be another parameter.
Instead of adding this here, where there will be duplicate calls, you can modify `/mermaid/packages/mermaid/src/mermaidAPI.ts` to add the `preprocessDiagram` call before `getDiagramFromText`.
How will that help ? Since the issue is when using `getDiagramFromText` API, the comments break for flowchart  so it needs to happen inside the `getDiagramFromText`
Thank you, made the change. Should be good to go

would be better.

I'll investigate and make necessary changes.

discussion_comments.comment_body: This change needs to be documented in a changeset. I'm not really sure if we should consider this as breaking or not, but it should atleast be a minor version bump. @aloisklink wdyt?

I don't see why it should be documented in a changeset.

Are we exposing the class types externally to any users of Mermaid?

The only thing I can exported that is potentially risky is the `InternalHelpers` type: https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/037ba2fa9cf40905bd1c35b1a450c5218a698f0f/packages/mermaid/src/internals.ts#L33, which looks to be used by the `@mermaid-js/layout-elk` package:

https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/037ba2fa9cf40905bd1c35b1a450c5218a698f0f/packages/mermaid/src/internals.ts#L15-L33

But it doesn't look like this `ClassNode` is being exported anywhere.
Although we are not exporting it, it's being used by others like excalidraw. So it's good to be documented in the changelog.

Ah, we are exporting it, just not via the `import XXX from 'mermaid';` entry-point.

We should probably ban `import type XXX from 'mermaid/dist/xxxxx'` by removing this line in our `package.json`: https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/d4e5acc530742c6bab3971cfe135137c774ae613/packages/mermaid/package.json#L14 (although this will be a breaking change), as otherwise every future change in the internal types will be a breaking change.

Maybe we can create a separate entry-point like `import type XXX from 'mermaid/internal-types'` where all these types are explicitly marked as not covered by SemVer and may change even between patch releases?

Or we could use something like <https://api-extractor.com/> to mark types as `@alpha`/`@beta`/`@internal` and warn us about breaking changes to the types automatically.
Should it? Agree with Alois that internal names should not be an issue that causes a version bump.

> This change needs to be documented in a changeset. I'm not really sure if we should consider this as breaking or not, but it should atleast be a minor version bump.

@sidharthv96 I was trying to create the changeset and running into an error.  Can you look into this for me?  I have not had power for 3 days, and current estimate is ~Monday (ack!), so will probably not be able to follow up on this again until then.
